title: 6H: authoritative curriculum map and coverage matrix
goal: Turn the four approved CIIP/PER research briefs and the immutable production plan into canonical source records, normalized curriculum notes, and a statement-level no-gap coverage matrix for the full official 5H–6H progression in Mathematics, Science, Geography, and History. Preserve the distinction between progression statements, explicit 6H endpoints, and unbanded end-of-cycle expectations.
acceptance: Canonical source records cite the current official CIIP/PER Cycle 2 version 3.0 (August 2024), institutional status pages, access dates, page references, and copyright/reuse caveats; no source PDF is committed.
Every progression statement listed in the four domain research briefs has a stable local statement ID, source trace, year-band classification, and one or more approved cluster mappings.
Every expectation explicitly marked 6e is labeled as a 6H endpoint; every unbanded expectation remains labeled end-of-cycle and is used only as directional or diagnostic evidence.

The matrix includes all 37 approved clusters and all 111 immutable exercise IDs from experiments/design/6h-production-plan.json, with exactly one construction, one practice, and one transfer-diagnosis lens per cluster.
A machine-readable matrix and a human-readable coverage report show zero unmapped in-scope progression statements; overlaps and cross-cluster statements are explicit rather than silently duplicated.
Geography and History follow PER context only, without an assumed canton layer or MER content presented as normative.
must_ask_if: The pinned decision, research, production-plan, or slice-graph inputs in experiments/design/6h-batch-inputs.json do not match the working baseline.
Primary CIIP/PER evidence contradicts a research brief or requires adding, removing, splitting, or merging one of the 37 approved clusters.
An official source is inaccessible and completeness would require an unsupported inference.
You need to reproduce substantial copyrighted curriculum text or copy an official PDF into the repository.
You need to change the immutable exercise ID allocation or files outside the declared areas.
